title: AtlasStudio Design Pattern
description: Machine-checkable representation of a Design Pattern Library document under studio/design-patterns/, extended for the inheritance model in studio/design-patterns/PATTERN_INHERITANCE_MODEL.md and studio/design-patterns/PATTERN_RESOLUTION_RULES.md. This schema formalizes the frontmatter fields a pattern's .pattern.md file carries; it does not replace the prose Layout/Composition/Passability/Event Rules sections required by studio/design-patterns/PATTERN_SCHEMA.md, which remain the authoritative full text. No existing pattern file's frontmatter is required to conform to this schema retroactively - it governs patterns authored under the inheritance model going forward. Validating an instance against this schema does not by itself resolve inheritance; see PATTERN_RESOLUTION_RULES.md for the resolution procedure a future Pattern Validator would run using these fields.

pattern_id.description: Stable, uppercase identifier for this pattern, e.g. PAT-INTERIOR-SHOP. Never reused for a different pattern, even after deprecation, per PATTERN_SCHEMA.md.

name.description: Human-readable pattern name, matching the name an 'Apply:' citation uses per PATTERN_APPLICATION_GUIDE.md.

version.description: MAJOR.MINOR version. See PATTERN_REVIEW_PROCESS.md for what triggers each kind of bump.

parent_pattern.description: The pattern_id of this pattern's single parent in the hierarchy defined by PATTERN_INHERITANCE_MODEL.md, or null if this pattern is currently a root for its branch (either a top-level category with no materialized environment-tier parent yet, or an environment-tier pattern itself). Single-inheritance only: a pattern has at most one parent. Absence of a parent does not mean the pattern is orphaned - see PATTERN_INHERITANCE_MODEL.md, 'Materialized vs. Virtual Nodes'.

category.description: The environment tier this pattern belongs to, per PATTERN_INHERITANCE_MODEL.md. Adding a new value here is a structural change to the hierarchy and should go through a dedicated work order, per that document's 'Future Extensibility' section - it is not a routine addition the way a new category or specialization pattern is.

confidence.description: Confidence level per PATTERN_CONFIDENCE_MODEL.md. Reported per pattern, never merged across a resolved inheritance chain - see PATTERN_RESOLUTION_RULES.md, 'Confidence Interactions'.

evidence_count.description: The number of genuinely independent corroborating sources behind this pattern's confidence claim, per PATTERN_CONFIDENCE_MODEL.md's bars. This is not simply the length of observed_sources: two floors of one building, or two zones of one shared map, count as one independent source, not two - see PATTERN_INHERITANCE_MODEL.md's discussion of interiors/chief-house.pattern.md and interiors/weapon-shop.pattern.md / interiors/armor-shop.pattern.md for worked cases where evidence_count is lower than len(observed_sources).

observed_sources.description: Every source this pattern cites as evidence - official sample map paths, accepted AtlasStudio build reports, or (per PATTERN_CONFIDENCE_MODEL.md's Low tier) a recorded project-preference decision. May be longer than evidence_count when sources are not mutually independent.

rule.description: The exact rule being deviated from, named or closely paraphrased from its source pattern - not merely a description of the difference in outcome, per PATTERN_RESOLUTION_RULES.md's 'Overrides'.

relationship.description: Which of PATTERN_INHERITANCE_MODEL.md's two override relationship types this is. vertical_override: source_pattern is this pattern's ancestor. cross_branch_clarification: source_pattern is not an ancestor (e.g. a sibling), and this pattern is stating that the named rule does not extend to its own domain.

source_pattern.description: pattern_id of the pattern the deviated rule originally comes from.

reason.description: Why this pattern deviates from the named rule.

exceptions.description: Explicit, named deviations from another pattern's rule, of either relationship type. An empty array means this pattern introduces no deviations from any other pattern. Every entry here is what a future Pattern Validator checks against the named source_pattern's own local_rules, per PATTERN_APPLICATION_GUIDE.md's 'Future Integration'.

applicable_conditions.description: The gating preconditions that must hold for this pattern to apply at all, per its Required Conditions prose section (PATTERN_SCHEMA.md). A specialization's applicable_conditions must satisfy every condition its parent states that it does not explicitly narrow further - it may tighten, never loosen, an inherited condition (PATTERN_INHERITANCE_MODEL.md, 'Specialization').

inherited_rules.description: Short labels identifying which of this pattern's parent's rules apply here unchanged (e.g. 'layout.threshold_construction', 'passability.region_free'). These are lightweight references into the parent pattern's own prose sections, not restated rule text - the full rule text lives once, in the ancestor pattern that states it, per PATTERN_INHERITANCE_MODEL.md's 'Shared Behavior'. Empty for a pattern with no parent.

local_rules.description: Short labels identifying the rules this pattern states itself - new rules its parent does not have, or rules listed in exceptions as overrides/clarifications of a parent or sibling rule. A specialization with no local_rules entries collapses onto its parent's guidance entirely, per PATTERN_INHERITANCE_MODEL.md's 'The Shop / Item Shop Double Duty'.

references.description: Paths to every document this pattern depends on or was cross-checked against, matching the prose References section required by PATTERN_SCHEMA.md.

applicable_genres.description: Which project genres this pattern is intended for, per PATTERN_SCHEMA.md. Optional here since it is not directly part of the inheritance/resolution mechanism, but retained for continuity with existing pattern frontmatter.

source_report.description: Path to the extraction report or corpus review this pattern's evidence was drawn from, per PATTERN_EXTRACTION_GUIDE.md's citation discipline. Optional here; observed_sources is the field this schema's inheritance-aware tooling actually consumes.

created.description: Date this pattern was first authored.

last_reviewed.description: Date this pattern's frontmatter was last confirmed accurate, per PATTERN_REVIEW_PROCESS.md.
